key_insight: The lead-free double perovskites K/Rb2SnGeX6 (X = Cl, Br, I) are predicted by DFT to be thermodynamically stable, mechanically ductile, and highly tunable from wide-gap absorber to thermoelectric material by halogen choice.
engineering_relevance: These materials are promising for safer photovoltaic absorbers, near-infrared optoelectronics, and high-ZT waste-heat recovery devices without using toxic lead.
method: DFT calculations of structure, elasticity, electronic bands, optics, and thermoelectric transport
Stability screening using formation energies, tolerance factors, and octahedral factors
Composition tuning across Cl/Br/I to map bandgap and thermal-transport trends
evidence_signal: The abstract reports direct bandgaps tunable from 1.44 eV to 0.64 eV and a peak thermoelectric performance of ZT = 2.4 for K2SnGeI6 at 1000 K.
watch_item: The reported performance is computational and high-temperature predictions should be validated experimentally, including synthesis, phase stability, and carrier-scattering behavior.
paper_title: First-Principles Study of Novel Lead-Free Double Perovskite \b{eta}2SnGeX6 (\b{eta} = K, Rb; X = Cl, Br, I) for thermomechanical, optoelectronic and outstanding thermoelectric applications
